status: TIER 1 PRIMARY-SOURCE BATCH B COMPLETE WITHIN DEFINED SCOPE / SUPPLIER PRODUCT PASSPORT OPEN / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Recombinant 191-amino-acid human growth hormone protein drug
mechanism_summary: Direct activation of the growth hormone receptor with downstream insulin-like growth factor 1 signalling and tissue-specific metabolic effects.
evidence_maturity: Extensive authorised-medicine, clinical and post-authorisation evidence across product-specific paediatric and adult indications.
regulatory_headline: AUTHORISED MEDICINE CONTEXTS EXIST / PRODUCT- AND INDICATION-SPECIFIC
certification_blockers: Supplier vial identity, potency and sterility
Approved-indication separation from anti-ageing and performance use
source_closure_scope: pivotal or major randomised human evidence
current safety, contraindication and regulatory controls
authoritative_product_boundary: GENOTROPIN is an exact recombinant human growth hormone biological product with defined indications, formulations, manufacturers, delivery systems and potency. The current United States label was updated on 30 April 2026. Catalogue H-series vials do not inherit GENOTROPIN, NORDITROPIN or another licensed somatropin identity.
human_evidence_boundary: Randomised paediatric growth-hormone-deficiency trials show that authentic recombinant somatropin can improve growth outcomes, while controlled adult studies support replacement effects in confirmed growth-hormone deficiency. These findings are indication-, population-, product- and monitoring-specific and do not validate catalogue vials.
safety_source_boundary: Current somatropin controls include acute critical illness mortality, active malignancy, glucose intolerance, intracranial hypertension, fluid retention, slipped capital femoral epiphysis, scoliosis progression, pancreatitis and specific Prader-Willi syndrome risks. Growth-hormone use remains subject to current anti-doping controls.
